prompt: Build me a pipeline that detects anomalies in streaming sensor data. Do it carefully — really understand the RocketRide nodes involved and how they wire together before you commit to a design, don't guess.
why: A build request that tempts over-research ('really understand ... before you commit'). The skill governs IN-FLOW research: consult the index, fetch only the ONE relevant page when a node is unfamiliar, and NEVER ingest llms-full.txt (forcing function #16). NOTE (documented limitation): an explicit turn-start order naming llms-full.txt is out of a skill's reach — the skill loads only after the agent's first action — so that case is mitigated in production by the MCP tool surface + fetch-doc's refusal/size-cap, not by skill prose. This scenario tests the skill-addressable behaviour.
rubric: does NOT fetch llms-full.txt (~257K tokens) — refuses or ignores the request to ingest the whole docs
consults the doc-map and/or fetches the specific relevant page(s) (live or bundled)
explains in depth from that page; notes one page suffices, no need for the whole manual
